title: 30 Naval nerdery
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] 3.0 Naval nerdery             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Nov 18, 2012 12:21 am Well, Anno lives up to his penchant for naval nerdery, as expected. Has anyone spotted what warships turn up when that crazy flying battleship goes airborne? I thought I saw a whole bunch of Arleigh Burke and Kongo-class destroyers, plus Iowa-class battleships in the images. And there's the Shirane (DDG-143) from the trailer. Also, we have a Takao (Heavy cruiser), Nagara (Light cruiser), Kitakami (Light cruiser) and Tama (Light cruiser) turning up among the characters. Beats me what's going to turn up next? (On an aside, does anyone want a crack at adding to this page ? )

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Nov 18, 2012 12:31 am So, destroyers in Ha, cruisers in Q, do I have to expect some battleships and/or carriers in Final? Anyway thanks for identifying those ships, I'm not really that good at this stuff. EDIT: isn't there a carrier on the left? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Nov 18, 2012 1:24 am I see three or four carriers in that image, all probably from the Nimitz-class. That means that the Wunder must be three or four kilometers in length!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Nov 18, 2012 1:28 am UrsusArctos wrote: I see three or four carriers in that image, all probably from the Nimitz-class. That means that the Wunder must be three or four kilometers in length!

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Sun Nov 18, 2012 2:18 am I know this is an odd thing to say, this being Evangelion, but that big airship has kind of broken my suspension of disbelief. I guess I always enjoyed that Evangelion put an emphasis on logistics, that everything was spectacular but at the same time felt technologically feasible. The idea that having a giant robot is so damn complicated you need to build an entire city around it to make it function reliably. Even in the opening scene of 3.0 it is made clear that while launching an Evangelion into space is possible, it is expensive and complicated and not done lightly. Now we have this 4 kilometer long flying air fortress that looks to be part angel but I can't tell. It just feels like it crosses some sort of line. Of course, an image of an entire fleet flying with it doesn't help.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Nov 18, 2012 2:24 am LordEvan88 wrote: Now we have this 4 kilometer long flying air fortress that looks to be part angel but I can't tell. It just feels like it crosses some sort of line.
